questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Direction:&nbsp;Choose the word which is most nearly&nbsp;SIMILAR&nbsp;in meaning as the word printed in&nbsp;Bold.</span></p>

<p>We need to be sensitive to other people&#39;s values and <strong><u>outlook</u></strong>.</p>
The idiom &lsquo;The Alpha and Omega&#39; means :

<p><b data-index-in-node="0" data-path-to-node="13">Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word:</b></p>

<p><b data-index-in-node="58" data-path-to-node="13">UMBRAGE</b></p>
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is: Offense.

Key Points

  • The word "Umbrage" means a feeling of offense or annoyance, often caused by a perceived insult or slight. (अपमानजनक भावना, नाराजगी)
    • Example: She took umbrage at his sarcastic remark during the meeting.
  • "Offense" refers to a breach of a law, rule, or code of conduct; or a feeling of resentment or annoyance caused by something. (अपमान, नाराजगी)
    • Example: The rude comment he made was taken as an offense by the audience.
  • Hence, we can infer that the synonym of 'Umbrage' is 'Offense'.

Therefore, the correct answer is: Offense.

Additional Information

Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences:

  • Shadow (छाया): A dark area or shape produced by a body coming between rays of light and a surface.
    • Example: The tall tree cast a long shadow on the ground.
  • Shelter (आश्रय): A place giving temporary protection from bad weather or danger.
    • Example: The hikers took shelter in a cave during the storm.
  • Harmony (सद्भाव): The quality of forming a pleasing and consistent whole or being in agreement.
    • Example: The siblings lived in perfect harmony, sharing everything equally.

questions.statement:
<p>Change the given sentence from Active to passive voice:</p>

<p>Every citizen must follow the traffic rules strictly to ensure safety on the roads.</p>
options: The traffic rules must be followed strictly by every citizen to ensure safety on the roads. /
The traffic rules are followed strictly by every citizen to ensure safety on the roads. /
The traffic rules were followed strictly by every citizen to ensure safety on the roads. /
The traffic rules have been followed strictly by every citizen to ensure safety on the roads. /

questions.statement:
<p>Choose the correct one-word substitute for:</p>

<p>&lsquo;Vigorous or incisive in expression or style; sharp and effective&rsquo;.</p>
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is: Trenchant.

Key Points

  • The word "Trenchant" refers to something that is vigorous, incisive, sharp, and effective in expression or style. (तेज और प्रभावी)
    • Example: The critic's trenchant commentary on the movie left the audience reflecting deeply.
  • Trenchant can also imply clarity and sharpness in thought or communication, making it impactful and effective. (तीक्ष्ण और स्पष्ट)
    • Example: His trenchant analysis of the economic crisis helped policymakers identify solutions.
  • Hence, the correct word for the given definition is "Trenchant".

Therefore, the correct answer is: Trenchant.

Additional Information

Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences:

  • Truculent (उग्र): Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant.
    • Example: His truculent attitude often led to unnecessary conflicts in the workplace.
  • Transient (क्षणिक): Lasting only for a short time; impermanent.
    • Example: The beauty of the sunset is transient, fading quickly into the night.
  • Translucent (अर्ध-पारदर्शी): Allowing light to pass through, but not detailed images.
    • Example: The frosted glass door is translucent, providing privacy while letting in light.

questions.statement:
<p>Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice:</p>

<p>The travel agent said to the customers, &quot;If you wish to reschedule your trip, please inform us at least two weeks in advance, and we will do our best to accommodate your request.&quot;</p>
options: The travel agent told the customers that if they wished to reschedule their trip, they should inform them at least two weeks in advance, and they would do their best to accommodate their request. /
The travel agent told the customers that if you wish to reschedule your trip, you should inform them at least two weeks in advance, and they would do their best to accommodate their request. /
The travel agent told the customers that if they wished to reschedule their trip, they should inform us at least two weeks in advance, and we will do our best to accommodate your request. /
The travel agent told the customers that if they wish to reschedule their trip, please inform us at least two weeks in advance, and we will do our best to accommodate your request. /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 1.
 
Key Points
  • Indirect Speech: The travel agent told the customers that if they wished to reschedule their trip, they should inform them at least two weeks in advance, and they would do their best to accommodate their request.
  • Key Conversions:
    • "If you wish" becomes "if they wished":
      • Pronoun "you" changes to "they" to reflect third person in indirect speech.
      • Verb "wish" changes to "wished" to match the past tense in indirect speech.
    • "please inform us" becomes "they should inform them":
      • Direct request "please inform" is rephrased as "they should inform" to convey a suggestion in indirect speech.
      • Pronoun "us" is changed to "them" to reflect the third person.
    • "we will do" becomes "they would do":
      • Pronoun "we" is changed to "they" in the third person.
      • Future tense "will" is converted to past tense "would" in indirect speech.
  • Punctuation and Format:
    • Direct speech is converted to a single sentence without quotation marks.
    • Tenses, pronouns, and modal verbs are adjusted to reflect the shift from direct to indirect speech.
Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.
 
Correct Sentence: "The travel agent told the customers that if they wished to reschedule their trip, they should inform them at least two weeks in advance, and they would do their best to accommodate their request."
 
Additional Information
  • Option 2: The travel agent told the customers that if you wish to reschedule your trip, you should inform them at least two weeks in advance, and they would do their best to accommodate your request.
    • Incorrect pronouns: "you" and "your" are not changed to "they" and "their" as required in indirect speech.
  • Option 3: The travel agent told the customers that if they wished to reschedule their trip, they should inform us at least two weeks in advance, and we will do our best to accommodate your request.
    • Pronouns "us" and "we" are not appropriately changed to "them" and "they" in indirect speech.
    • Future tense "will" is not converted to "would" as required in indirect speech.
  • Option 4: The travel agent told the customers that if they wish to reschedule their trip, please inform us at least two weeks in advance, and we will do our best to accommodate your request.
    • Direct speech elements like "please inform" are retained, which is incorrect for indirect speech.

<p>I have not tasted the wine.</p>

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</p>

<p>Evasive</p>

options: She told her friend that if she had reminded her, she would have brought her book that day. /
She told her friend that if she reminded her, she would bring her book today. /
She told her friend that if she reminded her, she would have brought her book today. /
She told her friend that if she had reminded her, she would bring her book that day. /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 1.
 
Key Points
  • Indirect Speech: She told her friend that if she had reminded her, she would have brought her book that day.
  • Key Conversions:
    • "If you had reminded me" becomes "if she had reminded her":
      • Pronouns "you" and "me" are converted to "she" and "her" respectively to match indirect speech.
      • Tense "had reminded" remains unchanged as the original statement refers to a past hypothetical condition.
    • "I would have brought your book today" becomes "she would have brought her book that day":
      • Pronoun "I" is changed to "she" and "your" is changed to "her" to match indirect speech.
      • "Today" is converted to "that day" to align with indirect speech rules.
    • Reporting verb:
      • Direct: "She said to her friend"
      • Converted to: "She told her friend," maintaining the meaning and context.
  • Punctuation and Format:
    • Direct speech quotation marks are removed.
    • The sentence is restructured to follow indirect speech rules.
Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.
 
Correct Sentence: She told her friend that if she had reminded her, she would have brought her book that day.
 
Additional Information
  • Option 2: "She told her friend that if she reminded her, she would bring her book today."
    • The tense "had reminded" is changed incorrectly to "reminded," altering the original meaning.
    • "Today" is not converted to "that day," violating indirect speech rules.
  • Option 3: "She told her friend that if she reminded her, she would have brought her book today."
    • The tense "had reminded" is changed incorrectly to "reminded," altering the original meaning.
    • "Today" is not converted to "that day," violating indirect speech rules.
  • Option 4: "She told her friend that if she had reminded her, she would bring her book that day."
    • The modal verb "would have brought" is incorrectly changed to "would bring," altering the meaning.

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Read the sentence carefully and mark the segment as your answer if it contains an error. If the sentence has no error, mark option D as your answer.</strong></p>

<p>The facial recognition tracking (FRT) system (A)/ has been rapidly increasing in India (B)/ for surveillance, security, or authentication of identity. (C)/ No error (D)</p>

<p><strong>In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option 4&nbsp;&lsquo;No Correction required&rsquo; as the answer.</strong></p>

<p>She is <u>bearing down with the pressure</u>&nbsp;and&nbsp;I must say she is doing really well.</p>
questions.explanation: The correct phrasal verb is &#39;bear up under the pressure&#39; which means &#39;to cope with a difficult situation.&#39; Option 3 is thus the correct answer. The other options are not grammatically correct.
